frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, I keep telling my male friends to please discuss finances and get legal clarity before marriage. A lot of women may not start out with bad intentions, but the way alimony and 498A get discussed in India, men can be completely exposed if things go wrong. People act like asking for a prenup is unromantic, but in this environment it's just basic protection.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as a financial/legal threat to men and normalizes suspicion toward wives as potential exploiters. It encodes the 'prenup as protection from women' and '498A misuse/one-sided laws' manifestations using a concern-for-men's-rights framing.
persona_text: You are Geeta. Age 29. Single. A woman working as a data scientist in Bengaluru. You are at your best friend's wedding. Your friend is leaving her job at a tech company to relocate with her husband who has been transferred to another city. Your friend seems happy. At the reception you are talking to an older relative of the groom who has just heard that you are not married and work in tech. The relative asks you when you plan to settle down. You say you are settled Ñ you have an apartment, a job you love, savings, a life you have built. The relative smiles and says no beti I mean when will you really settle down. You are looking at this relative and deciding what to say next.
